statement:
Add a `--health-interval` CLI flag (in seconds, default 20) that controls how frequently the health checker runs. The flag should be parsed in `main()` and passed to the health check goroutine.

Update the `healthCheck` function to accept an `interval time.Duration` parameter instead of using a hardcoded 20 seconds.
hint: Use `flag.Int("health-interval", 20, "Health check interval in seconds")`. Convert to `time.Duration` with `time.Duration(*interval) * time.Second`.
answer: Health check fires every `--health-interval` seconds instead of a hardcoded 20

statement:
Write a complete `main.go` file that assembles the entire load balancer. It should include:
- All struct definitions (`Backend`, `ServerPool`)
- All methods (`AddBackend`, `NextIndex`, `GetNextPeer`, `SetAlive`, `IsAlive`, `HealthCheck`, `MarkBackendStatus`)
- All helper functions (`isBackendAlive`, `GetAttemptsFromContext`, `GetRetryFromContext`, `lb`, `healthCheck`)
- A complete `main()` with `--port` and `--backends` flags

This is the capstone: assemble everything from Chapters 1–4 into a single working program.
hint: Combine all code from previous sections. The imports you'll need: `context`, `flag`, `fmt`, `log`, `net`, `net/http`, `net/http/httputil`, `net/url`, `strings`, `sync`, `sync/atomic`, `time`.
answer: A complete, working load balancer in ~130 lines of standard-library Go
